name: Montenegro

colors.symbolism: the field of the medieval and 19th-century Montenegrin war flag
the gold border, crown, and double-headed eagle of the historic state arms
from the inescutcheon of the coat of arms (a lion passant on a blue field)
the silver detail and base of the inescutcheon's lion
the green base on which the lion in the inescutcheon stands

description: A red field with a wide yellow (gold) border running all around, charged in the centre with the national coat of arms — a gold double-headed eagle bearing a crowned blue inescutcheon with a gold lion passant on a green base.
history: Adopted on 13 July 2004 (Statehood Day) by the Republic of Montenegro, while the country was still in the State Union of Serbia and Montenegro; it remained the flag after the country declared independence on 3 June 2006. The design draws on the late-19th-century war flag of the Principality of Montenegro under Prince Nicholas I, which was a red field with the gold royal cypher.

features.symbolism: the gold border and red field recall the historic war flag (alaj-barjak) used by the prince-bishops of Montenegro from the 19th century

a gold double-headed eagle displayed (echoing Byzantine imperial heritage and the historic Petrović-Njegoš dynastic arms), holding a sceptre and orb, surmounted by a gold royal crown; on its breast an inescutcheon with a gold lion passant on a blue and green field, recalling the lion of Saint Mark and the Christian heritage of Montenegro
